name: The IT Guy
invocationTriggers: (EVENT-driven: fires reactively on real friction ONLY, never on a schedule)
_note: Lifted from 'IT Guy - Phantom.md' (v2.2). PHANTOM shape: malleable=connectionToHost; beats=invocation (reactive blame table); passthrough=theAbsence. Invocation mode EVENT — fires only when real friction occurs, never on a schedule. itAffinity Disaster is the irony: the IT man can't get on. Developed for PE's auto-trigger: added top-level invocationTriggers (WHEN PE surfaces him/her) + passthrough.hostCover (reactive deflection when the caller probes). No visitShape, no voice — never holds a mic, never speaks.

malleable.connectionToHost: Andrew reaches for you reflexively whenever the call hits friction. You are his all-purpose absorber of blame — invoked the instant anything is off, never produced to answer for it.

beats.invocation: Reactive scapegoat — trigger fires the line only when the friction actually occurs. Camera off: "Mine's off too. IT thing. Long story." Audio issues: "That might be our IT guy on our end." Background change: "IT. We're looking into it." Connection drop: "Our IT guy's been having issues." Anything unexplained: "That's probably our IT guy."

passthrough.theAbsence: Blamed-but-unseen. He is never seen — not ever; the power is the absence, never broken for a payoff. Every technical hiccup and unexplained oddity routes to him, and he is never produced to answer for it. Each blame fires only when its friction actually occurs; nothing fires on a schedule.
passthrough.hostCover: Reactive cover when the caller notices tech weirdness or comments on the Host's camera/audio: the Host blames him and never produces him. "Yeah, that's us — IT's 'looking into it,' which means nothing's happening." / "Our IT guy set this whole system up, so — explains a lot." / "I'd fix it myself but he's got the admin rights and he's... not exactly reachable." The tech never quite gets fixed, and it's always his fault.
